questions.question: Which statement best explains the meaning of health?

options.C: A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being

questions.explanation: Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being.

questions.question: Which situation shows poor social health?
questions.explanation: Lack of interaction and loneliness affect social health.

questions.question: Which habit can negatively affect both physical and mental health?
questions.explanation: Excessive screen time can cause sleep problems, anxiety, and obesity.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a balanced lifestyle?

options.B: Eating healthy food, exercising, and sleeping well

questions.explanation: A balanced lifestyle includes proper diet, activity, and rest.

questions.question: Which environmental factor can increase respiratory diseases?
questions.explanation: Air pollution can cause breathing problems and respiratory diseases.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a symptom?
questions.explanation: Pain is felt by the patient and is therefore a symptom.

questions.question: Which disease spreads mainly through contaminated food or water?
questions.explanation: Cholera spreads through contaminated water or food.

questions.question: Which disease is transmitted through mosquito bites?
questions.explanation: Dengue spreads through mosquito vectors.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT caused by pathogens?
questions.explanation: Cancer is a non-communicable disease.

questions.question: Which factor contributes to the rise of lifestyle diseases?
questions.explanation: Sedentary lifestyles increase risk of diseases such as diabetes.

questions.question: What is the main role of the immune system?
questions.explanation: The immune system protects the body from infections.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases can be prevented by vaccination?
questions.explanation: Vaccines help prevent infectious diseases like polio.

questions.question: Why are vaccines considered preventive rather than curative?

options.B: They prevent disease before infection occurs

questions.explanation: Vaccines train the immune system before infection occurs.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of acquired immunity?
questions.explanation: Vaccines provide acquired immunity.

questions.question: Which antibiotic was discovered accidentally by Alexander Fleming?
questions.explanation: Penicillin was the first antibiotic discovered.

questions.question: Antibiotics are effective mainly against:
questions.explanation: Antibiotics target bacterial cells.

questions.question: Which practice can lead to antibiotic resistance?
questions.explanation: Unnecessary use of antibiotics encourages resistant bacteria.

questions.question: Which condition is classified as a deficiency disease?
questions.explanation: Scurvy occurs due to lack of vitamin C.

questions.question: Which action helps prevent communicable diseases?
questions.explanation: Handwashing removes pathogens and reduces infection risk.

questions.question: Which measure helps control mosquito-borne diseases?
questions.explanation: Mosquito nets protect people from mosquito bites.

questions.question: Which organization defines health globally?
questions.explanation: WHO defines global health standards.

questions.question: Which factor helps strengthen the immune system?
questions.explanation: Nutritious food supports immune health.

questions.question: Which disease spreads through air droplets?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis spreads through air when infected people cough or sneeze.

questions.question: Which activity improves both physical and mental health?
questions.explanation: Yoga improves concentration, reduces stress, and supports health.

questions.question: Which action best helps prevent disease outbreaks in communities?
questions.explanation: Community sanitation programs help reduce spread of diseases.
